claims.claim_text: Use specific exception types (IOError, ValueError, OSError) with descriptive messages via newException.
Catch errors only at the boundary where they become actionable — intermediate pipeline steps let exceptions propagate.
Use CatchableError as the catch-all for recoverable errors, not bare Exception.
For bool-return parse helpers, catch CatchableError once and return false.
Do not silently swallow exceptions — every except block does something useful.
Convert low-level errors at module boundaries using getCurrentExceptionMsg() for context.
claims.source: pdfocr/pipeline.nim (prepareFreshPayload catches IOError/ValueError and creates errorPageResult with getCurrentExceptionMsg)

claims.claim_text: Classify errors into typed enums for structured error reporting rather than passing raw messages.
Use enum-based error classification with descriptive messages for retry/failure decisions.
Use {.noinline.} on error-raising procs that format complex messages to avoid code bloat.
Translate C library error codes into Nim exceptions with human-readable descriptions.
claims.source: pdfocr/pdfium_wrap.nim (raisePdfiumError maps FPDF error codes), chunktts/sndfile_wrap.nim (raiseSndFileError uses sf_strerror)

claims.claim_text: Guard resource creation: check for nil/empty results and raise immediately.
Validate output from processing steps — raise if results are invalid (empty, zero-length, mismatched).
claims.source: pdfocr/pdf_render.nim (encodePageBitmap checks dimensions), chunktts/sndfile_wrap.nim (writeOpusFile checks zero chunks, decodeAudioFile checks read count)

claims.claim_text: Use try/finally for resource cleanup, not try/except.
Use typed error results (not exceptions) for pipeline item-level failures where the pipeline continues.
claims.source: pdfocr/pipeline.nim (PageResult with status/errorKind), chunktts/pipeline.nim (finalizeChunk with bool)

claims.claim_text: Distinguish retriable from final errors — classify before deciding retry vs. fail.
Raise at the point of failure with full context — include what operation failed and why.
Use specific except branches (IOError, ValueError) when different errors need different handling.
claims.source: pdfocr/pipeline.nim (prepareFreshPayload catches IOError and ValueError separately)

claims.claim_text: Gracefully handle missing/invalid config files — warn and continue with defaults rather than crash.
Do not add custom exception types unless callers handle them differently from existing types.
claims.source: all projects — no custom exception types defined anywhere

claims.claim_text: Prefer exception propagation over manual result-wrapper plumbing for recoverable errors.
Do not introduce ad-hoc result objects that pass only ok/kind/message between steps.
claims.source: contrast: PageResult/ChunkErrorKind are structured pipeline output, not plumbing

claims.claim_text: Use defer for cleanup of resources acquired in the same scope.
For retry loops: catch at the retry boundary, not inside the operation being retried.
On final failure after retries, raise a descriptive exception (not silently return).
claims.source: chunkvec/embeddings_client.nim (raises IOError or ValueError after max retries)

claims.claim_text: Catch multiple specific exception types separately when they need different recovery logic.
Guard against zero-length/empty inputs in procs that allocate or process data.
Use compileOption to select thread-safe vs single-threaded allocation at compile time.
claims.source: nim-ownership-hooks cowstrings reference — not error handling, omitted

claims.claim_text: Return structured results (with status enum) from pipeline orchestrators, not intermediate steps.
Never let exceptions escape a batch/orchestrator boundary — catch and record per-item.
Raise clear, bounded, actionable errors — include the operation name, the failure reason, and any relevant codes.
